key_insight: A benchmark of 1,800 clinical trial summaries showed that LLMs can be improved with knowledge-graph retrieval, but unsupported claims remain the main faithfulness risk.
clinical_relevance: For healthcare providers, patients, and payers, more faithful trial summaries could reduce misinformation when reviewing cancer-related evidence and treatment options.
method: Built a benchmark from 200 stratified ClinicalTrials.gov trials
Compared GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and Gemini 2.5 Flash across audience-specific prompts
Used a six-dimension faithfulness schema plus cross-encoder NLI scoring; tested a knowledge-graph-augmented retrieval system
evidence_signal: Across 1,800 generated summaries, unsupported claims were the dominant failure mode, and the retrieval system improved entailment and faithfulness with statistically significant gains (p < 0.0001).
watch_item: The gains were modest and model-dependent, so real-world deployment still needs rigorous validation and human review before use in high-stakes settings.
paper_title: Faithful by Design: Evaluating and Improving LLM-Generated Clinical Trial Summaries for Multi-Stakeholder Audiences
